parsed_topics.name: graph_intelligence
parsed_topics.description: The use of tree-sitter to parse code into dependency graphs and identify logical modules using graph algorithms like Leiden community detection and PageRank.

parsed_topics.name: git_intelligence
parsed_topics.description: Analysis of Git history to detect code hotspots, ownership patterns, and co-change relationships between files that are not captured by static imports.

parsed_topics.name: decision_intelligence
parsed_topics.description: Linking architectural decisions to graph nodes and tracking their staleness to provide institutional knowledge about codebases.

parsed_topics.name: documentation_generation
parsed_topics.description: Generation of LLM-powered wiki documentation with semantic search and freshness scoring to improve code understanding.

parsed_topics.name: context_retrieval_optimization
parsed_topics.description: Reducing the number of tokens needed per query by leveraging graph intelligence and co-change signals to focus on relevant code sections.

parsed_topics.name: cross_file_coupling
parsed_topics.description: Identifying relationships between files that do not have explicit import links but change together, particularly in the context of LLM behavior and session logs.

parsed_topics.name: local_storage_architecture
parsed_topics.description: Use of local storage systems like NetworkX for graphs, LanceDB for embeddings, and generated wiki pages to maintain intelligence layers without external dependencies.

parsed_topics.name: relevance_to_existing_projects
parsed_topics.description: Mapping repowise's features to existing projects like web-research, local LLMs, and Claude Code to identify potential integration points and benefits.

parsed_topics.name: model_selection_and_routing
parsed_topics.description: Discusses the selection and routing of models for different tasks, including the use of multiple models like qwen2.5-coder and qwen3 for code and prose respectively, along with production routing strategies.

parsed_topics.name: vramp_budget_constraints
parsed_topics.description: Addresses the constraints imposed by a 12GB VRAM budget on architectural decisions and model choices within the system.

parsed_topics.name: cross_repo_architecture
parsed_topics.description: Describes the cross-repository architecture involving three repositories, a shared hardware platform, and integration via MCP layer.

parsed_topics.name: prompt_engineering
parsed_topics.description: Details prompt decomposition strategies validated through empirical testing to find optimal stages for prompting.

parsed_topics.name: dpo_data_collection
parsed_topics.description: Explains how data for Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) is collected from verdict-labeled inference logs.

parsed_topics.name: retrieval_system
parsed_topics.description: Covers the retrieval system based on Latent Topic Graph (LTG), including its phases, reconciliation, and future plans for integration with chatbot and RAG systems.

parsed_topics.name: local_first_approach
parsed_topics.description: Outlines the principle of trying local models first before escalating to more powerful ones like Claude for judgment.

parsed_topics.name: documentation_and_knowledge_management
parsed_topics.description: Describes how documentation and knowledge are managed across different files and references, including the use of ref-indexing conventions.

parsed_topics.name: hybrid_retrieval
parsed_topics.description: The approach of combining multiple retrieval methods such as BM25, vectors, and graph-based search to improve accuracy and relevance. This pattern appears across several sources and is central to the refined architecture.

parsed_topics.name: precompiled_artifact
parsed_topics.description: The concept of pre-compiling sources into a persistent interlinked structure that can be queried multiple times. This pattern is key to reducing redundancy and improving efficiency in retrieval.

parsed_topics.name: graph_based_indexing
parsed_topics.description: Utilizing existing graph structures or building new ones from references and links to enable content linking and semantic navigation. This includes both wikilinks and typed knowledge graphs.

parsed_topics.name: hierarchical_scoping
parsed_topics.description: Organizing content into hierarchical scopes such as folders or rooms to enable scoped retrieval instead of flat search. This improves performance and relevance by narrowing down the search space.

parsed_topics.name: supersession_tracking
parsed_topics.description: Tracking when one piece of information supersedes another to handle stale or outdated content. This addresses the problem of drift in memory and ensures up-to-date knowledge.

parsed_topics.name: behavioral_edges
parsed_topics.description: Incorporating behavioral signals such as git co-change data to create edges in the knowledge graph. These edges capture relationships based on how content evolves together.

parsed_topics.name: domain_separation
parsed_topics.description: Organizing the system into distinct domains (e.g., profile, llm, web-research) with separate wikis and federated retrievers. This allows for modular and scalable knowledge management.

parsed_topics.name: filter_before_fetch
parsed_topics.description: Using IDs or summaries to filter out irrelevant content before fetching full details. This helps keep the context clean and efficient, especially for large models like Opus.

parsed_topics.name: structured_output
parsed_topics.description: The use of structured output formats like JSON schemas to ensure reliable and consistent model responses. This includes applying the 'format' parameter for structured outputs and defining JSON schemas for classification tasks.

parsed_topics.name: model_decomposition
parsed_topics.description: Breaking down complex tasks into smaller subtasks using one-task-per-prompt decomposition. This technique is applied across multiple layers to reduce complexity and improve output quality.

parsed_topics.name: temperature_tuning
parsed_topics.description: Adjusting model temperature settings per task or persona to control output randomness. Different temperatures are used for code/classification (0.1), general tasks (0.3), and creative tasks (0.7).

parsed_topics.name: few_shot_learning
parsed_topics.description: Incorporating few-shot examples to guide model behavior and improve performance. This includes using example libraries and injecting top-K examples per request for better task-specific responses.

parsed_topics.name: cascade_pattern
parsed_topics.description: Implementing a cascade pattern where local models are tried first and fallback to frontier models when necessary. This is used in routing decisions for classification and general task handling.

parsed_topics.name: best_of_n
parsed_topics.description: Generating multiple outputs and selecting the best one through evaluation. This technique is used in evaluator pipelines and multi-agent discussions to improve quality.

parsed_topics.name: memory_system
parsed_topics.description: Implementing a memory system that stores insights from interactions and retrieves relevant information for future prompts. This includes designing memory schemas and building read/write pipelines.

parsed_topics.name: persona_creation
parsed_topics.description: Creating specialized personas with defined roles, constraints, and formats. This includes designing skeleton system prompts and using persona-specific temperature settings.

parsed_topics.name: fine_tuning_pipeline
parsed_topics.description: Building pipelines for supervised and direct preference optimization fine-tuning using accumulated call logs. This includes SFT and DPO dataset builders and QLoRA training processes.

parsed_topics.name: persona_tiers
parsed_topics.description: Defines the two tiers of personas: Full and Bare. Full personas are standalone and include system prompts and full parameters, while Bare personas are wrappers for external tools that inject their own prompts.

parsed_topics.name: modelfile_structure
parsed_topics.description: Outlines the required and standard fields for Modelfile configuration. Includes FROM, PARAMETER fields like num_ctx and temperature, stop tokens, and SYSTEM prompt usage.

parsed_topics.name: system_prompt_skeleton
parsed_topics.description: Describes the standardized ROLE/CONSTRAINTS/FORMAT structure for system prompts in full personas. Specifies rules for writing effective constraints and output format descriptions.

parsed_topics.name: constraint_patterns
parsed_topics.description: Lists common patterns for writing effective constraints in system prompts, such as MUST output, MUST use, MUST include, MUST NOT add, MUST NOT invent, and MUST maintain.

parsed_topics.name: temperature_guide
parsed_topics.description: Provides guidance on selecting appropriate temperature values based on persona purpose: 0.1 for consistency, 0.3 for accuracy with variation, and 0.7 for creativity.

parsed_topics.name: model_selection
parsed_topics.description: Guides the selection of base models based on persona role, including recommendations for code generation, reasoning, classification, and writing tasks with associated VRAM and context requirements.

parsed_topics.name: naming_convention
parsed_topics.description: Defines the naming scheme for personas based on role and base model, distinguishing between Qwen2.5-based, Qwen3-based, and bare personas.

parsed_topics.name: persona_registration
parsed_topics.description: Explains how to register a persona with Ollama using the 'ollama create' command, creating a lightweight manifest that references shared base model weights.

parsed_topics.name: persona_creation_checklist
parsed_topics.description: Provides a step-by-step checklist for creating new personas, including model selection, temperature setting, system prompt writing, parameter comments, registration, testing, and registry updates.

parsed_topics.name: vram_budget_constraints
parsed_topics.description: This topic covers the constraints and implications of using a 12GB VRAM RTX 3060 as the primary hardware limitation for model selection and architecture decisions. It discusses how different model sizes fit within this budget and the performance trade-offs involved.

parsed_topics.name: model_tier_performance
parsed_topics.description: This topic details the empirical findings on model performance across different tiers (8B, 14B, 27B, 30B) in terms of token generation speed and output reliability. It emphasizes task-driven model selection rather than using the largest available model.

parsed_topics.name: prompt_decomposition
parsed_topics.description: This topic explores the strategy of breaking complex prompts into multi-stage processes to stay within model output budgets. It emphasizes that prompt decomposition is preferred over retries or larger context windows for handling complexity.

parsed_topics.name: cross_repo_architecture
parsed_topics.description: This topic outlines the architectural approach of separating functionality into three interconnected repositories (llm, expenses, web-research) that share a common hardware platform. It discusses integration through the MCP bridge server and overlay system.

parsed_topics.name: dpo_data_collection
parsed_topics.description: This topic describes the process of collecting data for Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) by logging model outputs and human verdicts. It emphasizes that this data collection happens passively during normal work rather than through dedicated annotation.

parsed_topics.name: local_first_with_frontier_escalation
parsed_topics.description: This topic establishes the principle of defaulting to local models for most tasks while escalating to frontier models (like Claude) only for specific complex or sensitive tasks. It describes how the MCP server facilitates this delegation.

parsed_topics.name: smart_rag_research
parsed_topics.description: This topic covers research into advanced retrieval techniques beyond traditional keyword/vector RAG. It identifies several philosophies for content-linking and proposes a Latent Topic Graph (LTG) as the architectural direction.

parsed_topics.name: latent_topic_graph
parsed_topics.description: This topic introduces the concept of a Latent Topic Graph (LTG) as a retrieval substrate that abstracts knowledge at the topic level rather than entity or chunk level. It describes its distinguishing properties and implementation plan.

parsed_topics.name: structured_output
parsed_topics.description: This topic advocates for using grammar-constrained decoding (via Ollama's format parameter) to reliably generate structured output like JSON. It warns against relying solely on prompt instructions for structured generation.

parsed_topics.name: pre_compiled_wiki_architecture
parsed_topics.description: The approach of pre-compiling sources into a persistent interlinked wiki before querying, which aligns with the 'build before HF push' constraint. This pattern is central to Karpathy's gists and repowise's generated wiki.

parsed_topics.name: hybrid_search_approach
parsed_topics.description: A retrieval strategy combining BM25, vector embeddings, and graph traversal to improve search accuracy and relevance. This pattern appears across multiple sources including Karpathy v2 and repowise.

parsed_topics.name: graph_based_retrieval
parsed_topics.description: The use of graph structures for retrieval, either through wikilinks (obsidian-mind) or typed knowledge graphs (Karpathy v2), emphasizing the exploitation of existing graph infrastructure.

parsed_topics.name: hierarchical_scoping
parsed_topics.description: Organizing information into hierarchical structures (rooms, folders, wings) to improve retrieval performance and reduce search space. This is validated by MemPalace and obsidian-mind approaches.

parsed_topics.name: filter_before_fetch
parsed_topics.description: Reducing token usage by filtering results before fetching full content, using IDs or summaries. This pattern is present in claude-mem and repowise approaches.

parsed_topics.name: supersession_tracking
parsed_topics.description: Tracking when one piece of information supersedes another, using typed links like 'supersedes:' or 'superseded-by:'. This helps manage stale memory in session logs and decision records.

parsed_topics.name: behavioral_signal_edges
parsed_topics.description: Using behavioral signals such as git co-change data to create edges in the knowledge graph. This idea is central to repowise's approach and adds value for code-related decision tracking.

parsed_topics.name: domain_specific_wikis
parsed_topics.description: Creating separate wikis per domain (e.g., profile+career, llm, web-research) to tailor pre-compilation rules and maintain scope-specific knowledge artifacts.

parsed_topics.name: llm_interaction
parsed_topics.description: Handles communication with the LLM designer model, including prompt construction, calling the model, and parsing responses. Manages structured output validation and error handling for LLM calls.

parsed_topics.name: persona_specification
parsed_topics.description: Defines the structure and validation rules for persona specifications. Includes schema definition, validation logic, and constraints on acceptable values for various persona attributes.

parsed_topics.name: prompt_engineering
parsed_topics.description: Constructs prompts for the LLM designer model with contextual information. Includes initial prompt building with codebase detection results and refinement prompts based on user feedback.

parsed_topics.name: codebase_detection
parsed_topics.description: Integrates with codebase analysis functionality to detect relevant persona patterns from existing code. Provides contextual information to the LLM designer based on detected frameworks or technologies.

parsed_topics.name: interactive_workflow
parsed_topics.description: Manages the interactive user experience including asking for descriptions, gathering feedback, and confirming actions. Handles both interactive and non-interactive modes of operation.

parsed_topics.name: validation_and_error_handling
parsed_topics.description: Validates persona specifications and handles errors during LLM interaction. Includes checks for required fields, valid values, and constraint limits with informative error messages.

parsed_topics.name: command_generation
parsed_topics.description: Converts persona specifications into command-line arguments for the downstream create-persona.py script. Maps internal spec values to appropriate flags and handles dry-run functionality.

parsed_topics.name: configuration_management
parsed_topics.description: Manages configuration constants and lookup tables for the persona builder. Includes model names, temperature mappings, domain choices, and other static configuration values.
